globalRules.followThrough: M marks always follow through. A marks follow through ONLY when isFollowThrough = true.

deduction.note: No deduction in any step scoring 0.

globalRules.allowFullMarksIfAnswerCorrect: A correct answer merits all marks unless a method is specified.
globalRules.fractionRule: All fractional answers must be simplified.

parts.label: a
parts.questionTextEn: Simplify $\dfrac{(x^{3} y^{-2})^{-2}}{(x^{-1} y)^{3}}$ and express your answer with positive indices.

parts.label: a
parts.questionTextZh: иЁӯ $x$ еҸҠ $y$ зӮәе…©еҖӢж•ёгҖӮ $x$ иҲҮ $y$ д№Ӣе·®зӮә $96$ пјҢиҖҢ $5$ иҲҮ $y$ зҡ„з©ҚзӮә $x$ гҖӮжұӮ $y$ гҖӮ
parts.questionTextEn: Let $x$ and $y$ be two numbers. The difference between $x$ and $y$ is $96$ while the product of $5$ and $y$ is $x$ . Find $y$ .

parts.label: a
parts.questionTextEn: Simplify $\dfrac{2}{x - 3} + \dfrac{5}{2x - 6}$ .

steps.displayText: $2x - 6 = 2(x - 3)$ , so the L.C.M. of the denominators is $2(x - 3)$

parts.label: a
parts.questionTextEn: Factorize $4m^{2} - 12m + 9$ .

steps.remark: 1M for using (a) and difference of two squares

parts.label: a
parts.questionTextEn: A lamp is sold at a discount of $25%$ on its marked price. After selling the lamp, the profit is $\$160$ and the percentage profit is $20%$ . Find the marked price of the lamp.

steps.remark: u-1 if the dollar sign is missing

parts.label: a
parts.questionTextEn: Consider the compound inequality $-3(x - 1) \ge x + 19$ or $5x < -10$ . Solve the compound inequality.

parts.label: b
parts.questionTextEn: Write down the greatest integer satisfying the compound inequality.

parts.label: a
parts.questionTextZh: й»һ $U$ еҸҠ $V$ зҡ„еқҗжЁҷеҲҶеҲҘзӮә $(-8, 6)$ еҸҠ $(5, -9)$ гҖӮ $U$ з№һеҺҹй»һ $O$ й ҶжҷӮйҮқж–№еҗ‘ж—ӢиҪү $90^\circ$ иҮі $U'$ гҖӮ $V'$ зӮә $V$ й—ңж–ј $y$ и»ёзҡ„еҸҚе°„еҪұеғҸгҖӮеҜ«еҮә $U'$ еҸҠ $V'$ зҡ„еқҗжЁҷгҖӮ
parts.questionTextEn: The coordinates of the points $U$ and $V$ are $(-8, 6)$ and $(5, -9)$ respectively. $U$ is rotated clockwise about the origin $O$ through $90^\circ$ to $U'$ . $V'$ is the reflection image of $V$ with respect to the $y$-axis. Write down the coordinates of $U'$ and $V'$ .

parts.label: b
parts.questionTextEn: Find the slope of $U'V'$ .

parts.label: a
parts.questionTextZh: еңЁең–дёҖдёӯпјҢ $ABCD$ зӮәеӣӣйӮҠеҪўпјҢе…¶дёӯ $AB = CD$ еҸҠ $\angle ABD = \angle CDB$ гҖӮиӯүжҳҺ $\triangle ABD \cong \triangle CDB$ гҖӮ
parts.questionTextEn: In Figure 1, $ABCD$ is a quadrilateral, where $AB = CD$ and $\angle ABD = \angle CDB$ . Prove that $\triangle ABD \cong \triangle CDB$ .

steps.displayTextZh: $AB = CD$ пјҲе·ІзҹҘпјүпјҢ $\angle ABD = \angle CDB$ пјҲе·ІзҹҘпјүпјҢ $BD = DB$ пјҲе…¬е…ұйӮҠпјү

parts.label: b
parts.questionTextZh: иӢҘ $\angle ADB = 27^\circ$ еҸҠ $\angle BCD = 94^\circ$ пјҢжұӮ $\angle ADC$ гҖӮ
parts.questionTextEn: If $\angle ADB = 27^\circ$ and $\angle BCD = 94^\circ$ , find $\angle ADC$ .

parts.label: a
parts.questionTextZh:
дёӢйқўзҡ„й »ж•ёеҲҶдҪҲиЎЁеҸҠзҙҜз©Қй »ж•ёеҲҶдҪҲиЎЁйЎҜзӨәжҹҗзө„еӯёз”ҹе®ҢжҲҗдёҖй …е°ҲйЎҢз ”зҝ’жүҖйңҖжҷӮй–“зҡ„еҲҶдҪҲгҖӮ
жүҖйңҖжҷӮй–“пјҲе°ҸжҷӮпјүпјҡ $10-14$ пјӣ $15-19$ пјӣ $20-24$ пјӣ $25-29$
й »ж•ёпјҡ $6$ пјӣ $x$ пјӣ $14$ пјӣ $4$
жүҖйңҖжҷӮй–“е°‘ж–јпјҲе°ҸжҷӮпјүпјҡ $14.5$ пјӣ $19.5$ пјӣ $24.5$ пјӣ $29.5$
зҙҜз©Қй »ж•ёпјҡ $6$ пјӣ $22$ пјӣ $36$ пјӣ $40$
еҜ«еҮә $x$ зҡ„еҖјпјҢдёҰжұӮи©ІеҲҶдҪҲзҡ„е№іеқҮж•ёгҖӮ
parts.questionTextEn:
The frequency distribution table and the cumulative frequency distribution table below show the distribution of the times taken to complete a project by a group of students.
Time taken (hours): $10-14$ ; $15-19$ ; $20-24$ ; $25-29$
Frequency: $6$ ; $x$ ; $14$ ; $4$
Time taken less than (hours): $14.5$ ; $19.5$ ; $24.5$ ; $29.5$
Cumulative frequency: $6$ ; $22$ ; $36$ ; $40$
Write down the value of $x$ and find the mean of the distribution.

parts.label: b
parts.questionTextEn: If a student is randomly selected from the group, find the probability that the time taken by the selected student to complete the project is less than $19.5$ hours.

parts.label: a
parts.questionTextZh: е·ІзҹҘ $f(x)$ жҳҜйғЁеҲҶйҡЁ $x^{2}$ иҖҢжӯЈи®ҠпјҢйғЁеҲҶеүҮйҡЁ $x$ иҖҢжӯЈи®ҠгҖӮе·ІзҹҘ $f(3) = 45$ еҸҠ $f(-2) = 10$ гҖӮжұӮ $f(x)$ гҖӮ
parts.questionTextEn: It is given that $f(x)$ partly varies as $x^{2}$ and partly varies as $x$ . Suppose that $f(3) = 45$ and $f(-2) = 10$ . Find $f(x)$ .

steps.displayText: Let $f(x) = h x^{2} + m x$ , where $h \neq 0$ and $m \neq 0$
steps.displayTextZh: иЁӯ $f(x) = h x^{2} + m x$ пјҢе…¶дёӯ $h \neq 0$ еҸҠ $m \neq 0$

parts.label: b
parts.questionTextEn: Write down the $x$-intercept(s) of the graph of $y = 6f(x)$ .

parts.label: c
parts.questionTextEn: Let $k$ be a real constant. Find the range of values of $k$ such that the equation $f(x) = k$ has two distinct real roots.

parts.label: a
parts.questionTextZh:
дёӢйқўзҡ„иҺ–и‘үең–йЎҜзӨәжҹҗзҗғйҡҠзҗғе“Ўзҡ„е№ҙйҪЎеҲҶдҪҲгҖӮ
иҺ–пјҲеҚҒдҪҚпјүпјҡ $1$ пјӣ и‘үпјҲеҖӢдҪҚпјүпјҡ $5$ пјҢ $7$ пјҢ $8$
иҺ–пјҲеҚҒдҪҚпјүпјҡ $2$ пјӣ и‘үпјҲеҖӢдҪҚпјүпјҡ $0$ пјҢ $3$ пјҢ $3$ пјҢ $3$ пјҢ $9$
иҺ–пјҲеҚҒдҪҚпјүпјҡ $3$ пјӣ и‘үпјҲеҖӢдҪҚпјүпјҡ $a$ пјҢ $6$ пјҢ $9$
иҺ–пјҲеҚҒдҪҚпјүпјҡ $4$ пјӣ и‘үпјҲеҖӢдҪҚпјүпјҡ $1$ пјҢ $b$ пјҢ $5$ пјҢ $7$ пјҢ $8$
е·ІзҹҘи©ІеҲҶдҪҲзҡ„дёӯдҪҚж•ёеҸҠеӣӣеҲҶдҪҚж•ёй–“и·қеҲҶеҲҘзӮә $31$ еҸҠ $21$ гҖӮжұӮ $a$ еҸҠ $b$ гҖӮ
parts.questionTextEn:
The stem-and-leaf diagram below shows the distribution of the ages of the players of a team.
Stem (tens): $1$ ; Leaf (units): $5$ , $7$ , $8$
Stem (tens): $2$ ; Leaf (units): $0$ , $3$ , $3$ , $3$ , $9$
Stem (tens): $3$ ; Leaf (units): $a$ , $6$ , $9$
Stem (tens): $4$ ; Leaf (units): $1$ , $b$ , $5$ , $7$ , $8$
It is given that the median and the inter-quartile range of the distribution are $31$ and $21$ respectively. Find $a$ and $b$ .

steps.displayTextZh: е…ұжңү $16$ еҖӢж•ёж“ҡпјӣдёӯдҪҚж•ё $= \dfrac{з¬¬ 8 еҖӢ + з¬¬ 9 еҖӢ}{2} = \dfrac{29 + (30 + a)}{2} = 31$

parts.label: b
parts.questionTextEn: A player now leaves the team. Is there any change in the mode of the distribution due to the leaving of the player? Explain your answer.

steps.displayText: The mode is $23$ , which occurs $3$ times ; every other age occurs at most once
After one player leaves, $23$ still occurs at least twice, so the mode remains $23$ : NO change

parts.label: c
parts.questionTextEn: If the range of the distribution is decreased, find the greatest possible standard deviation of the distribution.

steps.displayText: Range $= 48 - 15 = 33$ ; it decreases only if the player aged $15$ or the player aged $48$ leaves
Removing $15$ gives standard deviation $\approx 10.8$ , removing $48$ gives $\approx 10.8$ ; the greatest is $10.8$ (corr. to 3 sig. fig.)
steps.displayTextZh: з§»еҺ» $15$ еҫ—жЁҷжә–е·® $\approx 10.8$ пјҢз§»еҺ» $48$ еҫ— $\approx 10.8$ пјӣжңҖеӨ§иҖ…зӮә $10.8$ пјҲжә–зўәиҮідёүдҪҚжңүж•Ҳж•ёеӯ—пјү

parts.label: a
parts.questionTextZh: ең“ $C$ зҡ„ж–№зЁӢзӮә $x^{2} + y^{2} - 80x - 60y - 4556 = 0$ гҖӮд»Ҙ $G$ иЎЁзӨә $C$ зҡ„ең“еҝғгҖӮй»һ $H$ зҡ„еқҗжЁҷзӮә $(45, 42)$ гҖӮжұӮ $G$ иҲҮ $H$ д№Ӣй–“зҡ„и·қйӣўгҖӮ
parts.questionTextEn: The equation of the circle $C$ is $x^{2} + y^{2} - 80x - 60y - 4556 = 0$ . Denote the centre of $C$ by $G$ . The coordinates of the point $H$ are $(45, 42)$ . Find the distance between $G$ and $H$ .

parts.label: b
parts.questionTextZh: иЁӯ $P$ зӮә $C$ дёҠзҡ„дёҖеҖӢеӢ•й»һгҖӮз•¶ $\triangle GHP$ зҡ„йқўз©ҚжңҖеӨ§жҷӮпјҢжҸҸиҝ° $GH$ иҲҮ $GP$ д№Ӣй–“зҡ„е№ҫдҪ•й—ңдҝӮпјҢдёҰжұӮ $\triangle GHP$ зҡ„е‘Ёз•ҢгҖӮ
parts.questionTextEn: Let $P$ be a moving point on $C$ . When the area of $\triangle GHP$ is the greatest, describe the geometric relationship between $GH$ and $GP$ , and find the perimeter of $\triangle GHP$ .

steps.displayText: Area $= \dfrac{1}{2}(GH)(GP)\sin \angle HGP$ is greatest when $\sin \angle HGP = 1$ , i.e. $GH \perp GP$
steps.displayTextZh: йқўз©Қ $= \dfrac{1}{2}(GH)(GP)\sin \angle HGP$ ж–ј $\sin \angle HGP = 1$ жҷӮжңҖеӨ§пјҢеҚі $GH \perp GP$

parts.label: a
parts.questionTextEn: There are two solid metal spheres. The ratio of the volume of the smaller sphere to the volume of the larger sphere is $27 : 64$ . The radius of the larger sphere is $12 \text{ cm}$ . Express, in terms of $\pi$ , the surface area of the smaller sphere.

steps.displayText: Ratio of volumes $= 27 : 64 \;\Rightarrow\;$ ratio of radii $= 3 : 4$
Radius of the smaller sphere $= \dfrac{3}{4}(12) = 9 \text{ cm}$

parts.label: b
parts.questionTextZh: и©Іе…©зҗғиў«зҶ”и§ЈдёҰйҮҚй‘„жҲҗе…©еҖӢеҜҰеҝғжӯЈең“йҢҗпјҢиЁҳзӮәең“йҢҗ $A$ еҸҠең“йҢҗ $B$ гҖӮе·ІзҹҘ $A$ зҡ„й«ҳеҸҠеә•еҚҠеҫ‘еҲҶеҲҘзӮә $12 \text{ cm}$ еҸҠ $9 \text{ cm}$ гҖӮжҹҗеӯёз”ҹзҷјзҸҫ $B$ зҡ„еә•еҚҠеҫ‘зӮә $18 \text{ cm}$ пјҢдёҰиҒІзЁұ $A$ иҲҮ $B$ зӣёдјјгҖӮи©ІиҒІзЁұжҳҜеҗҰжӯЈзўәпјҹи©Ұи§ЈйҮӢдҪ зҡ„зӯ”жЎҲгҖӮ
parts.questionTextEn: The two spheres are melted and recast into two solid right circular cones. Denote these two circular cones by $A$ and $B$ . It is given that the height and the base radius of $A$ are $12 \text{ cm}$ and $9 \text{ cm}$ respectively. A student finds that the base radius of $B$ is $18 \text{ cm}$ . The student claims that $A$ and $B$ are similar. Is the claim correct? Explain your answer.

steps.displayText: If $A$ and $B$ were similar, the ratio of radii $= 18 : 9 = 2 : 1$ would give volume of $B = 8(324\pi) = 2592\pi \neq 2952\pi$ , so the claim is NOT correct
steps.displayTextZh: иӢҘ $A$ иҲҮ $B$ зӣёдјјпјҢеҚҠеҫ‘жҜ” $= 18 : 9 = 2 : 1$ жңғзөҰеҮә $B$ зҡ„й«”з©Қ $= 8(324\pi) = 2592\pi \neq 2952\pi$ пјҢж•…и©ІиҒІзЁұдёҚжӯЈзўә

parts.label: a
parts.questionTextZh: иЁӯ $p(x) = 3x^{3} + ax^{2} + bx - 24$ пјҢе…¶дёӯ $a$ еҸҠ $b$ зӮәеёёж•ёгҖӮз•¶ $p(x)$ йҷӨд»Ҙ $x - 1$ жҷӮпјҢйӨҳж•ёзӮә $3$ гҖӮе·ІзҹҘ $x + 2$ зӮә $p(x)$ зҡ„еӣ ејҸгҖӮжұӮ $a$ еҸҠ $b$ гҖӮ
parts.questionTextEn: Let $p(x) = 3x^{3} + ax^{2} + bx - 24$ , where $a$ and $b$ are constants. When $p(x)$ is divided by $x - 1$ , the remainder is $3$ . It is given that $x + 2$ is a factor of $p(x)$ . Find $a$ and $b$ .

parts.label: b
parts.questionTextEn: Is $x - 2$ a factor of $p(x)$ ? Explain your answer.

steps.displayText: $p(2) \neq 0$ , so by the factor theorem $x - 2$ is NOT a factor of $p(x)$

parts.label: c
parts.questionTextEn: Someone claims that the equation $p(x) = 0$ has two irrational roots. Do you agree? Explain your answer.

steps.displayText: For $3x^{2} + 10x - 12 = 0$ : $\Delta = 10^{2} - 4(3)(-12) = 244 > 0$ and $244$ is not a perfect square
So this quadratic has two irrational roots while $x = -2$ is rational ; the claim is AGREED

parts.label: a
parts.questionTextEn: There are $7$ men and $9$ women in a group. If $4$ people are randomly selected from the group to form a committee, find the probability that there are $2$ men and $2$ women in the committee.

parts.label: b
parts.questionTextEn: Find the probability that the number of men and the number of women in the committee are different.

steps.remark: 1M for using the complementary event / дҪҝз”Ёдә’иЈңдәӢд»¶

parts.label: a
parts.questionTextZh: иЁӯ $g(x) = 2x^{2} + 12kx + 20k^{2} + 6$ пјҢе…¶дёӯ $k$ зӮәйқһйӣ¶еҜҰеёёж•ёгҖӮеҲ©з”Ёй…Қж–№жі•пјҢд»Ҙ $k$ иЎЁзӨә $y = g(x)$ зҡ„ең–еғҸзҡ„й Ӯй»һзҡ„еқҗжЁҷгҖӮ
parts.questionTextEn: Let $g(x) = 2x^{2} + 12kx + 20k^{2} + 6$ , where $k$ is a non-zero real constant. Using the method of completing the square, express, in terms of $k$ , the coordinates of the vertex of the graph of $y = g(x)$ .

parts.label: b
parts.questionTextZh: еңЁеҗҢдёҖзӣҙи§’еқҗжЁҷзі»дёҠпјҢд»Ҙ $A$ еҸҠ $B$ еҲҶеҲҘиЎЁзӨә $y = g(x)$ зҡ„ең–еғҸзҡ„й Ӯй»һеҸҠ $y = 3g(-x)$ зҡ„ең–еғҸзҡ„й Ӯй»һгҖӮиЁӯ $M$ зӮә $AB$ зҡ„дёӯй»һгҖӮд»Ҙ $k$ иЎЁзӨә $M$ зҡ„еқҗжЁҷгҖӮ
parts.questionTextEn: On the same rectangular coordinate system, denote the vertex of the graph of $y = g(x)$ and the vertex of the graph of $y = 3g(-x)$ by $A$ and $B$ respectively. Let $M$ be the mid-point of $AB$ . Express, in terms of $k$ , the coordinates of $M$ .

parts.label: a
parts.questionTextZh: иЁӯ $c$ зӮәеҜҰеёёж•ёгҖӮж–№зЁӢ $x^{2} + cx - 12 = 0$ зҡ„ж №зӮә $\alpha$ еҸҠ $\beta$ гҖӮд»Ҙ $c$ иЎЁзӨә $\alpha^{2} + \beta^{2}$ гҖӮ
parts.questionTextEn: Let $c$ be a real constant. The roots of the equation $x^{2} + cx - 12 = 0$ are $\alpha$ and $\beta$ . Express $\alpha^{2} + \beta^{2}$ in terms of $c$ .

parts.label: b
parts.questionTextZh: жҹҗзӯүе·®ж•ёеҲ—зҡ„з¬¬ $1$ й …гҖҒз¬¬ $2$ й …еҸҠз¬¬ $3$ й …еҲҶеҲҘзӮә $c^{2}$ гҖҒ $\alpha^{2} + \beta^{2}$ еҸҠ $76$ гҖӮжұӮжңҖе°Ҹзҡ„ $n$ еҖјпјҢдҪҝи©Іж•ёеҲ—зҡ„йҰ– $n$ й …д№Ӣе’ҢеӨ§ж–ј $3 \times 10^{5}$ гҖӮ
parts.questionTextEn: The 1st term, the 2nd term and the 3rd term of an arithmetic sequence are $c^{2}$ , $\alpha^{2} + \beta^{2}$ and $76$ respectively. Find the least value of $n$ such that the sum of the first $n$ terms of the sequence is greater than $3 \times 10^{5}$ .

steps.displayText: 3rd term $= c^{2} + 48 = 76 \;\Rightarrow\; c^{2} = 28$ ; the 1st term is $28$

parts.label: a
parts.questionTextZh: еңЁең–дәҢдёӯпјҢдёүи§’еҪўзҙҷжқҝ $ABC$ иў«еӣәе®ҡпјҢдҪҝ $AB$ дҪҚж–јж°ҙе№іең°йқўдёҠгҖӮе·ІзҹҘ $AB = 24 \text{ cm}$ гҖҒ $AC = 20 \text{ cm}$ еҸҠ $\angle BAC = 105^\circ$ гҖӮжұӮ $BC$ гҖӮ
parts.questionTextEn: In Figure 2, the triangular paper card $ABC$ is held such that $AB$ lies on the horizontal ground. It is given that $AB = 24 \text{ cm}$ , $AC = 20 \text{ cm}$ and $\angle BAC = 105^\circ$ . Find $BC$ .

parts.label: b
parts.questionTextZh: иЁӯ $N$ зӮә $BC$ зҡ„дёӯй»һгҖӮжҹҗе·ҘеҢ зҷјзҸҫ $AC$ иҲҮж°ҙе№іең°йқўд№Ӣй–“зҡ„еӨҫи§’зӮә $60^\circ$ гҖӮи©Іе·ҘеҢ иҒІзЁұ $AN$ иҲҮж°ҙе№іең°йқўд№Ӣй–“зҡ„еӨҫи§’еӨ§ж–ј $35^\circ$ гҖӮи©ІиҒІзЁұжҳҜеҗҰжӯЈзўәпјҹи©Ұи§ЈйҮӢдҪ зҡ„зӯ”жЎҲгҖӮ
parts.questionTextEn: Let $N$ be the mid-point of $BC$ . A craftsman finds that the angle between $AC$ and the horizontal ground is $60^\circ$ . The craftsman claims that the angle between $AN$ and the horizontal ground exceeds $35^\circ$ . Is the claim correct? Explain your answer.

steps.displayText: Vertical height of $C$ above the ground $= AC \sin 60^\circ = 20 \sin 60^\circ \approx 17.32 \text{ cm}$
$B$ lies on the ground, so the height of $N$ (mid-point of $BC$) $= \dfrac{17.32}{2} \approx 8.66 \text{ cm}$
The horizontal distance from $A$ to $N$ is found to be $\approx 10.34 \text{ cm}$

parts.label: a
parts.questionTextZh: ең“ $C$ зҡ„ең“еҝғзӮәй»һ $G(30, 40)$ пјҢеҚҠеҫ‘зӮә $50$ гҖӮе·ІзҹҘй»һ $A(90, 120)$ дҪҚж–ј $C$ д№ӢеӨ–гҖӮ $AP$ еҸҠ $AQ$ еҲҶеҲҘзӮә $C$ еңЁй»һ $P$ еҸҠй»һ $Q$ зҡ„еҲҮз·ҡгҖӮжұӮйҖҡйҒҺ $A$ еҸҠ $G$ зҡ„зӣҙз·ҡзҡ„ж–№зЁӢгҖӮ
parts.questionTextEn: The centre of the circle $C$ is the point $G(30, 40)$ and its radius is $50$ . It is given that the point $A(90, 120)$ lies outside $C$ . $AP$ and $AQ$ are the tangents to $C$ at the points $P$ and $Q$ respectively. Find the equation of the straight line passing through $A$ and $G$ .

parts.label: b
parts.questionTextEn: Find the coordinates of the point of intersection of $AG$ and $PQ$ .

steps.displayText: $PQ \perp AG$ ; let $M$ be the intersection. In right-angled $\triangle GPA$ , $GM = \dfrac{GP^{2}}{GA} = \dfrac{50^{2}}{100} = 25$

parts.label: c
parts.questionTextEn: Find the equation of the inscribed circle of $\triangle APQ$ .

steps.displayText: $AP = AQ = PQ$ , so $\triangle APQ$ is equilateral and its in-centre is the centroid, lying on $AM$

parts.label: d
parts.questionTextEn: Someone claims that the ratio of the area of the inscribed circle to the area of the circumcircle of $\triangle APQ$ is $1 : 4$ . Do you agree? Explain your answer.

steps.displayText: For the equilateral $\triangle APQ$ the centroid, in-centre and circumcentre coincide at $(60, 80)$
Ratio of areas $= \pi(25)^{2} : \pi(50)^{2} = 1 : 4$ , so the claim is AGREED
